summary: Large fighter cleanup that replaced many raw ftCommon MotionState bitmask literals and numeric comments with named motion-state and move-id symbols. The PR introduced shared ftCo_MF_* motion flag constants in ftCommon/forward.h, used those constants throughout ftdata3.c, named two aerial attack move IDs, updated Peach float aerial entries to use the new names, and extended FtMoveId with additional unknown placeholders.
what_changed: Added a large block of static u32 const ftCo_MF_* ATTRIBUTE_USED motion-flag definitions to src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/forward.h.
Refactored src/melee/ft/ftdata3.c so MotionState entries use named ftCo_MF_* flags instead of raw hexadecimal flag words such as 0x00D00000, 0x00004066, and similar literals.
Updated ftData_MotionStateList comments from bare numeric indices like // 0 to enum-aware comments such as // ftCo_MS_DeadDown = 0.
Replaced motion-state move-id raw constants with symbolic FtMoveId_* shifts, for example FtMoveId_Unk01 << 24.
Renamed FtMoveId_Unk14 to FtMoveId_AttackAirF and FtMoveId_Unk17 to FtMoveId_AttackAirLw in src/melee/ft/forward.h.
Adjusted Peach float attack-air MotionState entries in ftPe_Init.c to use FtMoveId_AttackAirF << 24 and FtMoveId_AttackAirLw << 24 instead of the old unknown names.
Extended the FtMoveId enum with additional placeholders FtMoveId_Unk90 through FtMoveId_Unk94.
smart_moves: Factored repeated MotionState flag bit patterns into composable named constants, making the table searchable by behavior instead of only by hex value.
Built more specific ftCo_MF_* constants from shared base constants, for example attack, aerial attack, item, cliff, catch, throw, damage, down, and dead-state flag families reuse common components.
Kept unknown values explicit where not yet understood by preserving names like FtMoveId_Unk90 through FtMoveId_Unk94 instead of over-naming them.
Used ATTRIBUTE_USED on header-level static constants, which is a practical tactic for keeping symbolic constants available without creating unused warnings or forcing external storage.
Updated dependent character data, specifically Peach float aerial move IDs, at the same time as the enum rename to keep references consistent.
decomp_lessons: When a large motion-state table contains repeated bitmask literals, first extract shared flag combinations into named constants before attempting deeper semantic cleanup.
Use the common motion-state enum names in table comments to preserve index evidence while making entries easier to cross-reference with enum declarations.
Prefer symbolic FtMoveId_* << 24 expressions over raw packed move-id words; this documents the packed-field intent while preserving the table layout.
Only rename unknown enum values when the table context clearly identifies their meaning; otherwise keep numbered Unk placeholders.
Header-local static const flag aliases can be a low-risk way to improve readability across generated or data-heavy C tables without changing ABI-facing symbols.
naming_conventions: Common fighter motion flag constants use the prefix ftCo_MF_ followed by the motion/action family, such as ftCo_MF_Dash, ftCo_MF_Run, ftCo_MF_AttackAirF, ftCo_MF_ThrowHi, and ftCo_MF_Dead.
Shared or partially understood bitmask groupings may retain numeric suffixes, such as ftCo_MF_5_6, ftCo_MF_2_5_6, ftCo_MF_9_10, and ftCo_MF_3_5_6.
Common motion-state comments use the form // ftCo_MS_Name = index.
Move IDs use FtMoveId_* names and are packed in motion-state tables as FtMoveId_Name << 24.
Aerial attack move IDs follow the AttackAirN/F/B/Hi/Lw naming pattern; this PR specifically established FtMoveId_AttackAirF and FtMoveId_AttackAirLw.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced equivalent hexadecimal constants with symbolic constant expressions, preserving value semantics while improving maintainability.
Used composition of bitwise OR flag constants to encode exact original bitmasks without changing table structure.
Kept the packed move-id shift expression explicit, which helps avoid accidental byte/word-layout changes in MotionState initializers.
No direct assembly discussion or reviewer matching rationale was present in the PR slice.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue replacing raw MotionState flags with ftCo_MF_* constants.
Search for remaining FtMoveId_Unk* values that correspond to known attack or special move IDs.
Audit other character MotionState tables for raw packed move-id constants that could become FtMoveId_* << 24.
Check whether ftCo_MF numeric groupings like ftCo_MF_5_6 and ftCo_MF_9_10 were later given semantic names.
